summary:
The mandibular canine has been explicitly identified in the literature as carrying the lowest risk of developing periodontitis. Since Option B is the only choice that includes this specific tooth, it is selected as the correct response despite including a less favorable companion tooth like the lower premolar.  

(Note: This summary focuses solely on clinically significant facts from the provided text.) 

End of Summary.

We are asked to summarize ONLY the agent’s reasoning related to clinical points concerning which teeth are least involved in periodontitis.

Key point extracted: 
- Directly stated fact: "the risk of mandibular canine is least" among all teeth.
- Only Option B includes the mandibular canine.
- All other teeth referenced have either known increased risk (like molars/premolars) or no documentation supporting their low involvement.
- Therefore, although Option B does include some at-risk teeth, its unique content—the presence of the mandibular canine—is what makes it medically justified per existing evidence.

Final two-sentence concise version focusing exclusively on core clinical logic:

The mandibular canine has thelowest reported risk of developing periodontitis according to clinical evidence. Because no other option includes this tooth, Option B is the only valid choice grounded in proven low-risk dental anatomy.
